quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Homologous structures"?

options.text: The 4-carbon molecule that accepts acetyl-CoA to begin each turn of the Krebs cycle; regenerated at the end of each turn
Small intracellular molecules (cAMP, Ca²⁺, IP₃) that relay and amplify signals from the receptor to target proteins
Two rounds of nuclear division producing four haploid gametes (2n → n); includes crossing over and independent assortment for genetic diversity
Anatomical features in different species that share a common evolutionary origin but may serve different functions (e.g., vertebrate forelimbs)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Homologous structures" is defined as: Anatomical features in different species that share a common evolutionary origin but may serve different functions (e.g., vertebrate forelimbs). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Analogous structures"?

options.text: Normal cells stop dividing when they contact neighboring cells; cancer cells lose this control (contact inhibition)
Structures with similar function but different evolutionary origins, resulting from convergent evolution (e.g., bird wing vs. insect wing)
The end product of a metabolic pathway inhibits an early enzyme in the pathway, preventing overproduction (e.g., isoleucine inhibiting threonine deaminase)
The monomer of proteins: a central carbon bonded to an amino group (-NH₂), carboxyl group (-COOH), hydrogen, and variable R-group

quiz_questions.explanation: "Analogous structures" is defined as: Structures with similar function but different evolutionary origins, resulting from convergent evolution (e.g., bird wing vs. insect wing). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Vestigial structures"?

options.text: One gene affects the expression of another gene at a different locus (e.g., coat color in Labrador retrievers: B/b for color, E/e for deposition)
Reduced or functionless remnants of structures that were functional in ancestors (e.g., human appendix, whale pelvis bones)
Control points at G1, G2, and M where the cell evaluates conditions before proceeding; can halt the cycle if conditions are unfavorable
A cluster of prokaryotic genes under control of a single promoter and operator, transcribed as one polycistronic mRNA (e.g., lac operon, trp operon)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Vestigial structures" is defined as: Reduced or functionless remnants of structures that were functional in ancestors (e.g., human appendix, whale pelvis bones). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Fossil record"?

options.text: Cyclins accumulate and activate CDKs at specific cell cycle stages, driving progression through phosphorylation of target proteins
The proportion of a specific allele in a population: p = frequency of dominant allele, q = frequency of recessive allele; p + q = 1
Regulatory molecules bind to a site other than the active site, changing the enzyme's conformation and activity; can be activating or inhibiting
The chronological collection of fossils in rock strata documenting evolutionary changes over millions of years

quiz_questions.explanation: "Fossil record" is defined as: The chronological collection of fossils in rock strata documenting evolutionary changes over millions of years. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Molecular evidence"?

options.text: Benefits humans receive from ecosystems: provisioning (food, water), regulating (climate, flood control), cultural (recreation), and supporting (nutrient cycling, soil formation)
The proportion of a specific allele in a population: p = frequency of dominant allele, q = frequency of recessive allele; p + q = 1
DNA and protein sequence comparisons that reveal evolutionary relationships; more similar sequences indicate more recent common ancestry
A mathematical model estimating a population's probability of persistence over a specified time period, incorporating demographic and environmental variability

quiz_questions.explanation: "Molecular evidence" is defined as: DNA and protein sequence comparisons that reveal evolutionary relationships; more similar sequences indicate more recent common ancestry. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Anatomical features in different species that share a common evolutionary origin but may serve different functions (e.g., vertebrate forelimbs)"?

options.text: Parental vs. recombinant
DNA polymerase III
Angiogenesis
Homologous structures

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Homologous structures".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Structures with similar function but different evolutionary origins, resulting from convergent evolution (e.g., bird wing vs. insect wing)"?

options.text: Gel electrophoresis
Analogous structures
Entropy
Allele frequency

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Analogous structures".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Reduced or functionless remnants of structures that were functional in ancestors (e.g., human appendix, whale pelvis bones)"?

options.text: Final electron acceptor
Species richness
Mitosis
Vestigial structures

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Vestigial structures".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The chronological collection of fossils in rock strata documenting evolutionary changes over millions of years"?

options.text: Proton-motive force
Molecular clock
Meiosis
Fossil record

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Fossil record".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "DNA and protein sequence comparisons that reveal evolutionary relationships; more similar sequences indicate more recent common ancestry"?

options.text: r-selected species
Cellular respiration (overall)
Molecular evidence
Gel electrophoresis

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Molecular evidence".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Evolution and Evidence is accurate?

options.text: Exponential growth (dN/dt = rN) occurs when resources are unlimited, producing J-shaped curves.
DNA polymerase's proofreading (3'→5' exonuclease) and mismatch repair maintain a remarkably low error rate (~1 error per 10⁹ bases).
Evolution and Evidence establishes that populations change over time through multiple converging lines of evidence.
Phylogenetics reconstructs evolutionary relationships using shared derived characters (synapomorphies) organized in cladograms or phylogenetic trees.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Evolution and Evidence as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Evolution and Evidence?

options.text: The specific bond orientation (α vs. β linkages) determines digestibility and function: α-1,4 bonds in starch are digestible by humans, while β-1,4 bonds in cellulose are not.
When damage is too severe, p53 activates apoptosis to prevent propagation of mutations that could lead to cancer.

The fossil record shows transitional forms (Tiktaalik between fish and tetrapods), comparative anatomy reveals homologous structures (bat wing, whale flipper, human arm sharing the same bone pattern), molecular biology shows DNA/protein sequence...
Parsimony (fewest evolutionary changes) and maximum likelihood methods are used to determine the most probable tree topology.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Evolution and Evidence as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Evolution and Evidence?

options.text: Genetic mapping constructs a linear order of genes along chromosomes based on recombination frequencies.
Energy and Metabolism describes how cells manage energy through coupled reactions and metabolic pathways.
Evolution is the unifying theory of biology.

Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Evolution and Evidence as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Evolution and Evidence?

options.text: Biodiversity and Conservation addresses the variety of life at genetic, species, and ecosystem levels and the threats to it.
Environmental factors (temperature, pH, salt concentration) also affect enzyme activity.
DNA Replication and Transcription describes how genetic information stored in DNA is copied (replication) and read into mRNA (transcription).
Evolution and Evidence establishes that populations change over time through multiple converging lines of evidence.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Evolution and Evidence as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Evolution and Evidence?

options.text: The three modes of natural selection are directional (shifts the mean), stabilizing (reduces variation around the mean), and disruptive (favors extremes).
Speciation occurs through geographic isolation (allopatric) or reproductive isolation within the same area (sympatric).
Phylogenetics reconstructs evolutionary relationships using shared derived characters (synapomorphies) organized in cladograms or phylogenetic trees.

The fossil record shows transitional forms (Tiktaalik between fish and tetrapods), comparative anatomy reveals homologous structures (bat wing, whale flipper, human arm sharing the same bone pattern), molecular biology shows DNA/protein sequence...

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Evolution and Evidence as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Evolution and Evidence?

options.text: Ice insulation of lakes in winter: because ice (density 0.917 g/cm³) floats, aquatic organisms survive below the frozen surface
Forensic genetics using Mendelian inheritance patterns to establish paternity or identify family relationships from DNA evidence
Whale evolution documented through a remarkable fossil series: Pakicetus (land mammal) → Ambulocetus (walking whale) → Dorudon (aquatic) → modern whales over ~50 million years
Exercise physiology: the Krebs cycle's rate increases during aerobic exercise as increased ATP consumption lowers ATP/ADP ratio, relieving allosteric inhibition

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Evolution and Evidence.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Evolution and Evidence?

options.text: Antibiotic resistance in bacteria as observable evolution: mutations conferring resistance are selected for in the presence of antibiotics, producing resistant populations within months
Active transport of ions (Na⁺/K⁺ ATPase) using ATP to pump 3 Na⁺ out and 2 K⁺ in per cycle, maintaining the resting membrane potential essential for nerve impulses
Human population growth following a roughly exponential pattern from 1 billion (1800) to 8 billion (2022), with demographic transition slowing growth in developed nations
Industrial enzyme applications: proteases in detergents, amylases in food processing, and DNA polymerase in PCR for forensics and diagnostics

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Evolution and Evidence.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Evolution and Evidence applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Metabolic interconnections: Krebs cycle intermediates serve as precursors for amino acid synthesis, fatty acid synthesis, and gluconeogenesis
Darwin's finches on the Galápagos Islands illustrating adaptive radiation: 14 species with different beak shapes evolved from a single ancestor to exploit different food sources
Virus tracking during COVID-19 pandemic using SARS-CoV-2 genome sequencing to build phylogenetic trees tracing transmission chains and identifying new variants
Feedback inhibition in the cholesterol synthesis pathway: statins mimic the substrate of HMG-CoA reductase, acting as competitive inhibitors to lower cholesterol levels

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Evolution and Evidence.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Evolution and Evidence?

options.text: Stem cell differentiation controlled by sequential gene regulation, with the same genome producing >200 different specialized cell types in the human body
Industrial enzyme applications: proteases in detergents, amylases in food processing, and DNA polymerase in PCR for forensics and diagnostics
Cyanide poisoning blocking Complex IV of the ETC, preventing electron transfer to O₂ and halting all aerobic ATP production
Whale evolution documented through a remarkable fossil series: Pakicetus (land mammal) → Ambulocetus (walking whale) → Dorudon (aquatic) → modern whales over ~50 million years

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Evolution and Evidence.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Evolution and Evidence in practice?

options.text: Genetic engineering using the universal genetic code to express human insulin in E. coli bacteria, producing recombinant insulin for diabetes treatment
Antibiotic resistance in bacteria as observable evolution: mutations conferring resistance are selected for in the presence of antibiotics, producing resistant populations within months
DNA fingerprinting using STR analysis for forensic identification, with Combined DNA Index System (CODIS) databases matching crime scene samples to individuals
Cancer as uncontrolled mitosis: mutations in proto-oncogenes and tumor suppressors enable cells to bypass cell cycle checkpoints

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Evolution and Evidence.
